description_long: This is a sleek, blue and gold military jet aircraft with 'US NAVY' markings and the 'Blue Angels' insignia on its fuselage. The airplane features a streamlined fuselage, sharply swept-back wings with yellow-tipped edges, and a single vertical stabilizer at the tail. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and is enclosed with a clear canopy, likely accommodating a single pilot. The jet is equipped with tricycle landing gear, with the main gear under the wings and a nose wheel beneath the cockpit. The engine exhaust is located at the rear, and the overall design emphasizes aerodynamic efficiency and high-speed performance. The surface appears smooth and metallic, with prominent insignia and text for identification.
description: A blue and gold US Navy jet with swept-back wings, single vertical stabilizer, and a forward enclosed cockpit, marked with 'Blue Angels' insignia.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, cockpit, landing gear, and prominent 'US NAVY' and 'Blue Angels' markings.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the jet exhaust, swept-back wings, and the single vertical stabilizer.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the fuselage, cockpit, landing gear, and mirrored 'US NAVY' markings.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the pointed nose, cockpit canopy, and the angular sweep of the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with sharply swept-back wings and no visible winglets; moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with one nose wheel and two main gear units.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
